ORDER ON JUDGMENT CREDITOR MATTHEW OUYANG'S MOTION FOR AN ASSIGNMENT ORDER HEARD ON MARCH 22, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 506, HON. PAUL SLAVIT PRESIDING. IT IS ORDERED THAT JUDGMENT DEBTOR MICHAEL MURPHY'S RIGHTS TO RECEIVE PAYMENT ARE HEREBY ASSIGNED TO JUDGMENT CREDITOR MATTHEW OUYANG - SAID ASSIGNMENT TO INCLUDE 25% OF JUDGMENT DEBTOR'S NON-EXEMPT FUTURE EARNINGS; ANY AND ALL RIGHTS TO RECEIVE PAYMENT IN CONNECTION WITH SETTLEMENTS, JUDGMENTS OR CAUSES OF ACTION; AND ANY OTHER NON-EXEMPT ASSETS OR INCOME.

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: 9:12am
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Judge Pro Tem Sharon O'Grady presiding, clerk-Beth Wilber, Dept. 525:
Respondent, Matthew Ouyang, appearing through counsel, Steve Williams, Esq.; Appellant, Michael S. Murphy, appearing through counsel, Mark Ohmer Rorem. Parties and witnesses are sworn.
Counsel present their respective cases through examination and cross-examination.
Mr. Williams calls Gene Rolen, process server who is examined and cross-examined by counsel.
Judge O'Grady questions both counsel. The matter matter is submitted. Judge O'Grady rules from the bench that the motion is denied. No evidence was introduced or retained by the Court.
